commentary:
# Cross-Exchange Liquidity Commentary – 2026-07-21

The US equity venues dominate the liquidity hierarchy, with NYSE and Nasdaq 100 commanding substantially lower Amihud costs (0.0057% per $1M and 0.0042% per $1M respectively) and comparable volumes exceeding $206M and $308M. Asia-Pacific exchanges occupy the middle tier: Tokyo leads this segment with an ILLIQ of 0.0337% per $1M and notably high turnover of 2.9657%, while Hong Kong and Korea offer contrasting profiles—Hong Kong shows tight spreads at 0.6585% but modest turnover at 0.4312%, whereas Korea exhibits severe price impact (0.1373% per $1M) and wide spreads at 1.5166%. The European cluster reveals an important divergence: Euronext posts tight spreads of 0.6224% and low ILLIQ at 0.0291% per $1M on comparable volumes of $9.3M, yet its 0.3782% turnover lags peer-comparable venues, suggesting pricing efficiency without corresponding trade velocity. By contrast, London Stock Exchange trades 8.8% higher volumes but incurs double the price impact (0.0770% per $1M) and widest spreads at 0.7856%, indicating structural liquidity constraints. SIX Swiss and Xetra complete the laggard cohort with minimal comparable volumes ($3.2M and $4.0M) and higher transaction costs.
